question_title: Will Discord publicly file an S-1 prospectus on SEC EDGAR before May 31, 2026?
summary: Discord Inc. has NOT filed an S-1 on SEC EDGAR as of May 18, 2026 (verified via direct EDGAR search). With only 12 days remaining until the May 30 deadline, no news of an imminent filing, the March 2026 target already missed, age verification compliance delays pushing to H2 2026, a large valuation gap in secondary markets, and Polymarket probabilities declining sharply, the probability of a public S-1 appearing by May 30 is very low. The factor sum implies an even lower probability, but factors are correlated (multiple aspects of the same delay narrative), so I set the final estimate at 4% to account for the small possibility of a surprise filing given that the confidential process has been underway since January with top-tier underwriters.

factors.description: No S-1 on EDGAR as of May 18, 2026 - verified via 4 independent SEC EDGAR queries. Zero filings found for Discord Inc. (CIK 0001763840).
Only 12 calendar days remain until May 30 deadline. Public S-1 filing typically happens 2-4 weeks before IPO pricing, meaning the decision would need to already be made with no visible signals.
March 2026 target already missed - established pattern of delay despite confidential filing in January.
Age verification compliance delay pushed to H2 2026 after February backlash, requiring amended SEC filing with material risk data before going public.
Valuation gap - secondary market at ~$7.23B (down 51.8% from $15B peak); IPO roadshow would need institutional buyers at large premium over current trading.
Polymarket IPO market cap probabilities plummeting: from 13% on May 11 to 5.8% on May 18, indicating strong market consensus against near-term IPO.
Discord did confidentially file in Jan 2026 with Goldman Sachs/JPMorgan as underwriters - the process IS underway, just delayed. SEC review could theoretically be complete after 4.5 months.
No news reports of imminent public S-1 filing or roadshow preparation from any major financial outlet.

reasoning:
## Forecast: Discord S-1 Filing by May 31, 2026

**Probability: 4% (NO is overwhelmingly likely)**

### Key Evidence:
1. **Verified: Zero S-1 filings on EDGAR** as of May 18, 2026 (confirmed via 4 independent SEC EDGAR queries)
2. **Only 12 days remain** until the May 30 deadline
3. **March 2026 target already missed** — the originally rumored debut window passed without action
4. **Age verification compliance** pushed to H2 2026, requiring amended SEC filing
5. **Prediction markets declining sharply** — Polymarket IPO probability fell from 13% to 5.8% in one week
6. **No news signals** of imminent public filing or roadshow preparation

### The only YES scenario:
Discord surprises with an unannounced public S-1 filing in the next 12 days, despite zero advance signals and multiple structural headwinds. While technically possible (the confidential process has been underway since January), this would be unprecedented for a high-profile IPO of this size.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Discord publicly file an S-1 prospectus on SEC EDGAR before May 31, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43533)
[Discord IPO Closing Market Cap Predictions & Odds 2026 | Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/event/discord-ipo-closing-market-cap)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, at any time between January 1, 2026 and 11:59 PM ET on May 30, 2026, a Form S-1 (or S-1/A) registration statement filed by Discord Inc. (CIK 0001763840) appears publicly on SEC EDGAR. The resolver will check Discord's EDGAR filings page: https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=0001763840&type=S-1&dateb=&owner=include&count=40 — if one or more rows with Filing Type "S-1" or "S-1/A" are listed with a Filing Date on or before May 30, 2026, the question resolves YES. Otherwise it resolves NO.
fine_print: A previously-confidential DRS (Draft Registration Statement) that has not been made public on EDGAR does NOT count; only filings publicly visible as Form S-1 or S-1/A on EDGAR count. If Discord is filing under a different parent entity name but the SEC EDGAR system links the S-1 to Discord's business (e.g., the registrant's prospectus identifies Discord Inc. as the issuer of the registered securities), that filing counts. If SEC EDGAR is unavailable on the resolution date, the resolver may use SEC EDGAR's full-text search (https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22Discord+Inc%22&forms=S-1) or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) confirming a public S-1 filing.

input.anchor: Base rate for confidential S-1 becoming public within specific 12-day window when original timeline already passed: ~10% (generous estimate given the process is underway but delayed)
input.assessment:
This question asks whether Discord will publicly file an S-1 on EDGAR by May 30, 2026. The evidence overwhelmingly points to NO:

1. VERIFIED: No S-1 exists on EDGAR as of May 18 (direct SEC search confirms zero results)
2. Only 12 calendar days remain (May 18 to May 30)
3. The original March 2026 target was missed - a pattern of delay
4. Multiple structural obstacles: age verification delays pushed to H2 2026, large valuation gap, weak unit economics
5. Prediction markets are pricing the IPO probability very low and declining rapidly
6. No news reports suggest an imminent public filing

The only scenario for YES is a surprise filing in the next 12 days with zero advance signals - extremely unlikely given the headwinds and that such a filing would typically be preceded by media reports of an imminent roadshow. The confidential filing from January confirms intent, but intent without execution in the remaining window is insufficient.

The Kalshi "Before June 1" at 31% appears stale relative to Polymarket's rapidly declining probabilities (5.8% on May 18 for any market cap bracket). Even the Kalshi price likely reflects the broader range of before-June filing scenarios rather than just S-1 appearance on EDGAR.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor at ~10% (generous for 12-day window given delays). Factors push strongly toward NO. Final estimate of 4% accounts for small possibility of surprise filing. Hedging check: not hedging toward 50% - evidence is strongly directional.
input.key_uncertainties: Could Discord file the public S-1 without advance media reports? Theoretically possible but unprecedented for a company this high-profile. Could market conditions suddenly become so favorable that Discord accelerates?
input.tool_audit: EDGAR direct search (via fetch_url): Confirmed zero S-1 filings - critical verification. Research sub-agent: Provided thorough EDGAR analysis. Web search: Found prediction market data (Polymarket, Kalshi) and expert analysis. ActionNetwork fetch: Provided excellent Kalshi analysis with specific probability estimates. Forge Global: 403 access denied. All key data sources returned useful results.
input.update_triggers: 1. News reports of Discord scheduling roadshow dates would shift dramatically toward YES. 2. Any SEC EDGAR activity on CIK 0001763840 beyond Form D filings. 3. Reports of Discord resolving age verification compliance concerns earlier than expected.
input.process_reflection: Good workflow for a time-sensitive binary question. The EDGAR direct verification was the most important data point and was easy to obtain. Prediction market integration was smooth - Polymarket data came through search results with structured API data. The research sub-agent provided comprehensive EDGAR analysis which was the foundation for the forecast. Minor friction: one research sub-agent didn't have access to live web search, reducing its value for the timeline question.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
**Summary:** The agent's 4% probability estimate for Discord publicly filing an S-1 on SEC EDGAR before May 31, 2026 is well-supported by the evidence chain. No material errors found.

**Factor verification against trace:**

1. **Factor 1 (No S-1 on EDGAR, logit=-2.5):** Verified. The trace shows a direct EDGAR fetch (`efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22Discord+Inc%22&forms=S-1`) returning zero results, and the research sub-agent cross-validated through 4 independent EDGAR queries. Confidence 1.0 is justified.

2. **Factor 2 (12 days remain, logit=-1.5):** Verified. Published May 18, deadline May 30 = 12 calendar days.

3. **Factor 3 (March 2026 target missed, logit=-1.0):** Verified. TechCrunch and Bloomberg articles in web search results confirm Discord targeted March 2026 and missed it.

4. **Factor 4 (Age verification delay to H2 2026, logit=-1.0):** Verified. ActionNetwork article mentions "Discord postponed age verification rollout to H2 2026 after February backlash" requiring amended SEC filing. IBTimes results also confirm. Confidence 0.7 is appropriate given this is third-party analysis.

5. **Factor 5 (Valuation gap ~$7.23B vs $15B, logit=-0.5):** Verified. ActionNetwork article states "Secondary market prices Discord at $7.23B (down 51.8% from 2021's $15B)."

6. **Factor 6 (Polymarket probabilities declining, logit=-1.0):** Minor inaccuracy — the factor says "from 15% to 5.8%" but the trace's Polymarket data shows 13% on May 11 to 5.8% on May 18 (the 15.4% peak was May 12). The direction and conclusion are correct; not materially misleading.

7. **Factor 7 (Confidential filing Jan 2026, logit=+0.5):** Verified. Bloomberg, TechCrunch, Reuters all confirm in web search results.

8. **Factor 8 (No news of imminent filing, logit=-1.0):** Verified. All web searches returned no reports of imminent public S-1 filing or roadshow preparation.

**Double-counting:** Factors 6 (Polymarket) and 8 (no news) have mild overlap (both reflect market/media perception of no imminent filing), but they measure different signals (quantified market price vs. absence of news reports). Not strict double-counting. The agent also recognized factor correlation in its reflection, noting the raw factor sum (implying 0.07%) was unrealistically low due to correlated evidence, and correctly adjusted upward to 4%.

**Wrong-direction factors:** All negative factors have negative logits; Factor 7 (positive indicator of process being underway) correctly has a positive logit.

**Resolution criteria alignment:** The agent directly checked the exact EDGAR URL specified in the resolution criteria (CIK 0001763840, S-1 type), and the assessment explicitly engages with the "publicly visible on EDGAR" requirement. Good alignment.

**Counterargument quality:** The counterargument constructs a coherent scenario (4.5 months of SEC review could be complete, public filing can happen in a single day, Kalshi at 31%). It identifies specific triggers that would change the estimate. This is a genuine attempt at adversarial reasoning, not a token gesture.

**Probability assessment:** The 4% estimate is consistent with available market data: Polymarket prices Discord IPO by June 30 at ~5.8% (broader window, for actual IPO not just S-1 filing); ActionNetwork expert analysis estimates true pre-July probability at 20-25% (well below market price), implying pre-June would be significantly lower. With zero S-1 on EDGAR and 12 days remaining, 4% is within a defensible 3-6% range. I find no meaningful divergence from what the evidence supports.

**Tool audit accuracy:** The agent reports "Forge Global: 403 access denied," which matches the 1 error in 14 calls (7.1% error rate) shown in tool metrics. Accurate.

**Confidence self-assessment:** 0.75 is reasonable — there's some uncertainty about whether a surprise filing could emerge, but the core evidence (zero EDGAR filings, declining markets) is very strong.
